task_completion.description: Verify that the three source FITS files (673nmos.fits, 656nmos.fits, 502nmos.fits) are opened in an image processing application.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Windows, tabs, or layer panels showing the loaded FITS images with their filenames (673nmos, 656nmos, 502nmos) visible in the interface.

task_completion.description: Verify that the FITS files are assigned to the correct RGB channels according to the Hubble Palette.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A color composite dialog, channel merge tool, or layer blending setup showing 673nmos.fits assigned to Red, 656nmos.fits assigned to Green, and 502nmos.fits assigned to Blue.

task_completion.description: Verify that brightness, contrast, or histogram stretching adjustments are applied to make the 'Pillars of Creation' clearly visible.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A histogram, curves, or brightness/contrast adjustment dialog open, with the image canvas showing the distinct pillar structures of the Eagle Nebula becoming clearly visible and not overly dark.

task_completion.description: Verify that a combined RGB color image of the Eagle Nebula has been successfully generated.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A single image window showing a full-color composite of the Eagle Nebula, typically displaying the characteristic golden/brown and blue hues of the Hubble Palette.

task_completion.description: Verify that the final composite image is saved as a PNG to the specified processed directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' or 'Export' dialog showing the directory '/home/ga/AstroImages/processed/' and the filename 'eagle_nebula_hubble_palette.png', or a terminal/file manager confirming the file exists at this exact path.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent used a graphical image processing application rather than just running a pre-written script in the terminal to bypass the manual creation process.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the GUI of an image editing/processing software (like AstroImageJ, GIMP, etc.) being actively used to manipulate the images.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent actually combined the channels and adjusted the image locally, rather than downloading a pre-made Hubble Palette image of the Eagle Nebula from the internet.
integrity.visual_evidence: Presence of intermediate steps like individual grayscale FITS files open, channel mapping dialogs, and histogram adjustments, rather than a web browser downloading a final image.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent used the local source files provided in the task directory.
integrity.visual_evidence: File open dialogs or window titles should indicate the files were loaded from '~/AstroImages/eagle_nebula/'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output path is exactly '/home/ga/AstroImages/processed/eagle_nebula_hubble_palette.png'. The standard Hubble Palette mapping must be strictly followed: 673nmos.fits (representing [SII]) mapped to the Red channel, 656nmos.fits (representing H-alpha) mapped to the Green channel, and 502nmos.fits (representing [OIII]) mapped to the Blue channel. The target is the Eagle Nebula (M16), which features the highly recognizable 'Pillars of Creation'.
